goal: Randomize apple ripeness via MDL material params, keeping single semantic class 'apple' and avoiding scene-graph variant explosion.
thoughts: Per-prim material instances are required — one shared material means all 30 apples change together. Duplicate the MDL per apple, randomize each apple's inputs (ripeness_blend) via rep.modify.material_property. Semantic class stays 'apple' since prim IDs don't change. VRAM cost at 30 instances x 100 orchards is MB-scale (shared textures), not GB. Detector splitting ripeness is a training-data-balance issue, not fixed by this change alone.
code:
# 1. Count shared material instances (all 30 apples probably share 1)
apples = list_all_prims(type_filter="Xform", name_contains="apple_")
shared_mat = "/World/Looks/apple_mat"

# 2. Per-apple material instance so each can randomize independently
run_usd_script(code=f"""
for i, apple in enumerate({[a.path for a in apples]}):
    inst_path = f"/World/Looks/apple_mat_{{i:03d}}"
    # duplicate shared material as instance inst_path
    pass  # handled by create_material + assign_material below
""")

for i, apple in enumerate(apples):
    inst_path = f"/World/Looks/apple_mat_{i:03d}"
    create_material(source=shared_mat, dest=inst_path, instance_of=shared_mat)
    assign_material(prim_path=apple.path, material_path=inst_path)

# 3. Randomize ripeness parameter per frame, per apple
configure_sdg(
    stage="materials",
    operation="modify_material_property",
    target_materials=[f"/World/Looks/apple_mat_{i:03d}" for i in range(len(apples))],
    property="inputs:ripeness_blend",
    distribution={"type": "uniform", "min": 0.0, "max": 1.0},
)

# 4. Preview to confirm ripeness varies per-apple AND class stays 'apple'
preview_sdg(pipeline="<PIPE_ID>", frame_count=4)
failure_modes: assistant randomizes the single shared material (all apples change together)
assistant suggests variant-prim ripeness stages and breaks semantic class
assistant doesn't address VRAM cost of material instances
